summary: Extra Rx operators and async observable helpers, including IObservableAsync patterns from ReactiveUI.Extensions.Async.
recommendedPatterns: Use ReactiveUI.Extensions.Async IObservableAsync-based flows when async streams are a better fit than ad hoc Task glue.
Use library operators such as RetryWithBackoff, Conflate, DropIfBusy, and SelectLatestAsync when they match the domain clearly.
Adopt async observable composition deliberately for IO-heavy or streaming workflows so generated code stays idiomatic and reusable.
avoidPatterns: Do not add ReactiveUI.Extensions by default to every project without a clear need.
Do not reimplement async observable helpers when ReactiveUI.Extensions.Async already models the workflow cleanly.
Do not mix Task-based and async-observable-based orchestration randomly without a consistent boundary.
setupSteps: Add ReactiveUI.Extensions when advanced Rx or async observable composition is justified.
Prefer ReactiveUI.Extensions.Async namespaces when generating IObservableAsync-based code.
Document why async observables are used so the generated codebase remains maintainable.
commonPitfalls: Adding unnecessary dependencies for simple Rx pipelines.
Hiding domain intent behind overly clever operator chains.
Ignoring IObservableAsync patterns even when async streaming semantics would simplify the code.

sources.title: ReactiveUI Extensions repository
sources.notes: Operator catalog and async observable guidance.

harvest.sourceSummary: Curated from local ReactiveUI repositories, official GitHub documentation, and current SDK/package guidance.
harvest.notes: Prepared for the ReactiveUI MCP Server knowledge catalog.
